project_overview.project_name: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Human Capital Investment Project
project_overview.project_development_objective: To improve availability, utilization, and quality of primary healthcare services and elementary education services in Selected Districts of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
project_overview.total_financing: US$200 million equivalent (SDR 100.6 million Credit + SDR 45.7 million Grant)
project_overview.beneficiaries: Direct beneficiaries: individuals from host communities and refugees in Peshawar, Nowshera, Haripur and Swabi districts. Indirect beneficiaries: workers hired for civil works, health and education officers, managers and staff. Specific focus on elementary and secondary school-age children, women of reproductive age, and girls from both refugee and host communities

geographic_scope.coverage_type: regional (4 districts in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province)
geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: partial - KP province has been impacted by crisis and tribal areas integration; however, incidents of crisis have significantly reduced. The province hosts 822,429 Afghan refugees (38% of national refugee population), with 519,303 (63% of KP refugees) in the four target districts. Districts are dealing with COVID-19 pandemic impacts

key_components.component_name: Component 1: Improving Delivery of Quality Primary Health Care Services
key_components.description: Strengthening PHC infrastructure, equipment and supplies at BHUs and RHCs; establishing healthcare facility networks with 24/7 CEmONC services; supporting MNCH services, NCD prevention and management; health worker training and capacity building; governance and management strengthening including disease surveillance systems and quality improvement

key_components.component_name: Component 2: Improving Availability and Quality of Education Services
key_components.description: Adding and equipping classrooms; upgrading primary schools to middle schools and middle to secondary schools; enhancing teaching and learning quality through teacher training, PITE functional review, student assessment systems; strengthening contract management and implementation support

key_components.component_name: Component 3: Strengthening Community Engagement and Accountability
key_components.description: Enhancing community engagement through health promotion grants, health promoting schools, parent-teacher council grants; establishing grievance redress mechanisms; community-based advocacy and awareness campaigns; strengthening data utilization for decision-making

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Department of Health (Component 1 and Sub-component 3.1) and Elementary and Secondary Education Department (Component 2 and Sub-component 3.2) of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa. Department of Communication and Works (C&W) executes civil works. Project Management Units (PMUs) established in each implementing agency
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Project Steering Committee (PSC) led by Additional Chief Secretary Planning and Development with representatives from Finance, Health, Education, and Commissioner for Afghan Refugees. Project Technical Committees in each department meeting quarterly. District-level coordination through District Education Officers (DEOs) and District Health Officers (DHOs) under oversight of Deputy Commissioners
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: Works procurements managed by C&W Department using e-bidding system (transitioning to digital measurement books with geotagging). Goods and services procured by respective departments. Framework contracts for repetitive procurements. Blockchain technology for pharmaceutical supply chain management. World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF Borrowers applied. Project Procurement Strategy for Development (PPSD) prepared
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: Detailed M&E framework with PDO and intermediate results indicators. Data collected by Independent Monitoring Units (IMUs) of Health and Education Departments. Bi-annual Interim Unaudited Financial Reports (IUFRs) and annual audited financial statements. Bi-annual implementation support missions with technical assistance as needed. Midterm review at 25 months. Social assessments incorporated in monitoring. Specialized surveys for refugee data collection coordinated with Commissioner for Afghan Refugees and UNHCR

identified_risks.risk_description: KP province integrating tribal areas creating social and political pressure; unstable situation in Afghanistan affecting border areas and potential refugee influx; incidents of crisis have reduced but remain a concern
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project design accommodates geographical scope changes if required; strong government commitment to reform and social sector prioritization; ongoing World Bank support

identified_risks.risk_description: COVID-19 pandemic weakening economic stabilization efforts and structural reforms; projected fiscal deficit contraction affecting development spending; economic growth uncertainty
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Government commitment to staying the course supported by World Bank, IMF and ADB; IMF approved US$1.4 billion Rapid Financing Instrument; debt suspension initiative providing fiscal space; focus on pro-poor growth and human capital protection

identified_risks.risk_description: Department of Health and E&SED have very limited experience with WB-financed operations; frequent staff turnover in implementing units; weak procurement and financial management capacity; two implementing units may add coordination delays
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Use of existing structures; support from KP Shared Services Unit; additional technical assistance provided as needed; strong PMUs with dedicated fiduciary staff; role of PSC essential for coordination; representation of Finance and Planning in PSC

identified_risks.risk_description: Weak procurement and financial management implementation capacity in both departments; limited experience with large procurements; E&SED has very limited foreign-funded project experience; civil works delegated to C&W but coordination challenges possible
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Two separate PMUs with strong fiduciary staff recruitment; blockchain technology for pharmaceutical procurement; civil works delegated to C&W Department; framework contracts for repetitive procurements; dedicated Procurement Committee; close oversight and regular monitoring

identified_risks.risk_description: Large project scope requiring sound dialogue and complementarity between line departments and C&W; expansion and fine-tuning of existing interventions; coordination challenges between multiple implementing units
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project activities aligned with provincial education and health strategies; complementary with other donor activities (DfID); many interventions expand existing programs; strong PSC coordination; regular technical committee meetings

identified_risks.risk_description: Stigma towards refugees despite generally positive host-refugee relationships; strong resistance from social norms to gender gap closing activities; economic shocks from COVID-19 straining structural deficiencies; GBV and VAC prevalent in KP with reporting challenges
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Component 3 includes specific interventions to address these issues; community engagement through established groups (BISP beneficiaries); grievance redress mechanisms for GBV and VAC reporting; community-based advocacy campaigns; activities targeting girls' education and women's health access

identified_risks.risk_description: Risk that elements of Pakistan's Protection Framework could be changed due to evolving regional situation, impacting project objectives and RSW funding eligibility
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Ensuring Protection Framework remains adequate as confirmed by UNHCR; coordination with World Bank's prospective support under Strengthening Institutions for Refugee Administration (SIRA); regular monitoring of framework adequacy

identified_risks.risk_description: Minor civil works in health and education facilities generating temporary environmental impacts (dust, noise, waste, contamination); occupational health and safety issues; water quality concerns; potential small-scale land acquisition
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: ESMF, EHCWMP, and RPF prepared; framework approach for facility selection; mitigation measures for construction impacts; health care waste management outsourcing to private companies; voluntary land donation process; site-specific RAPs if needed; grievance redress mechanisms

safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - Two-tier Grievance Redress Mechanisms (district and provincial level) established for each implementing agency before commencement of civil works. Mechanisms provide avenue for project stakeholders to raise and resolve grievances including those related to GBV, VAC, and land issues. Additional channels include Education Hotline, Consumer Satisfaction Surveys, mobile/smart phones, social and traditional media, and community-level interactions. World Bank's Grievance Redress Service (GRS) and Inspection Panel also available
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Project aims to reduce gender disparities in education and improve women's access to quality healthcare. Specific measures include: increasing school spaces for girls and bringing schooling closer to home; supporting community schools, distance education, and girls' community schools; campaigns to mobilize parents on girls' education importance; female health practitioners' visits to girls' schools; BISP beneficiary committees engagement; PTC grants focusing on safe learning environments for girls; separate toilets for men and women in health facilities; female teachers recruitment for girls' schools; gender-disaggregated indicators throughout results framework
vulnerable_groups: Afghan refugees (1.4 million in Pakistan, 822,429 in KP, 519,303 in target districts)
Girls and women (facing mobility constraints and social norms barriers)
Daily wage workers and informal sector workers affected by COVID-19
Refugee women with limited livelihood opportunities
Children at risk of dropping out due to economic shocks

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project explicitly addresses conflict and fragility through: (1) inclusive approach focusing on both host communities and refugees to sustain protection environment; (2) support for Pakistan's Protection Framework for Afghan refugees; (3) alignment with Solutions Strategy for Afghan Refugees (SSAR) and Repatriation and Management Policy (RMP); (4) activities designed to reduce vulnerability and build resilience; (5) community engagement mechanisms to address social tensions; (6) grievance redress mechanisms for conflict-sensitive issues; (7) coordination with UNHCR and Commissioner for Afghan Refugees; (8) medium-term system strengthening approach contributing to sustainability of crisis response
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: KP province classified as 'Fragile within a non-fragile Country'. Key fragility factors: (1) Protracted refugee situation with 1.4 million Afghan refugees (38% of national total) in Pakistan, 822,429 in KP; (2) Integration of tribal areas creating social and political pressure; (3) Unstable situation in Afghanistan affecting border areas; (4) COVID-19 pandemic impacts on economy and service delivery; (5) Low human capital accumulation (HCI of 40%); (6) High poverty rates (27.6% in KP); (7) Large gender gaps in education (boys 87% vs girls 71% participation); (8) Weak health and education systems; (9) Limited livelihood opportunities for refugees; (10) Vulnerability to climate-induced disasters
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Project includes COVID-19 response elements aligned with GoKP response plan; support for makeshift hospitals and ICU upgrades for COVID-19 cases; health worker training for COVID-19 critical care; community awareness campaigns on COVID-19 prevention; distance education modalities to maintain schooling during disruptions; health promotion messaging on GBV risks during pandemic; construction activities are small-scale (not requiring large labor influx) reducing GBV risk; security considerations in facility design and operations; biometric systems for health worker tracking; standardized signage for facility visibility and accountability
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - Project explicitly targets refugee populations. Pakistan hosts 1.4 million Afghan refugees (nearly half are women), with 822,429 in KP (38% of KP population in target districts). Key refugee-related aspects: (1) Refugees access education and health services same as residents; (2) Approximately 35% of refugee households severely impacted by COVID-19 pandemic; (3) Refugee girls have 34% gross enrollment rate vs 87% for males; (4) Refugee women face constrained mobility and limited livelihood opportunities; (5) Project targets four districts hosting 63% of KP's refugee population; (6) Facility selection based on refugee concentration; (7) Project funded through IDA 18 Regional Sub-Window for Refugees and Host Communities (US$125 million of US$200 million total); (8) Coordination with UNHCR and Commissioner for Afghan Refugees; (9) Specialized surveys for refugee data collection; (10) Activities benefit both refugees and host communities without distinction
